mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is valid when the policy rate trend is falling, and it fails if equity returns do not improve after rate declines.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average after FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal's Rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals weakening household income and demand, which lowers S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. The effect should be strongest when unemployment is clearly accelerating rather than merely drifting.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broad nominal demand and labor income growth, supporting corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when both series improve together rather than separately.
failure_condition: Reject if joint expansions in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not outperform single-series expansions, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline in the top joint-quartile states.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO signals stronger industrial output and supply-side activity, which supports cyclical earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect fails when industrial production rises only because of temporary restocking without follow-through in output growth.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum is not followed by above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the relationship flips for more than one full rolling year.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains low, real rates fall and the market reads policy as still accommodative, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is conditional on inflation being the dominant mover rather than a simultaneous policy tightening response.
failure_condition: Reject if CPI shocks under low FEDFUNDS do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A fall in HOUST after a prior upswing signals weakening housing demand and tighter credit transmission, which depresses cyclical sentiment and S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is a downside warning only when housing activity rolls over from an elevated level.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for 2 consecutive months after being above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing rollovers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return is not statistically below zero.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE identifies genuine labor market deterioration that reduces earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is stronger when payroll losses and unemployment gains occur together.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor deterioration state does not predict weaker 60-day SPX returns than either component alone, or if the sign is positive in the majority of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, improving INDPRO alongside stabilizing UNRATE signals that supply is normalizing without a labor-market breakdown, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if industrial recovery occurs while unemployment is still rising sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO with stable or falling UNRATE does not outperform baseline 20-day returns, or if the relationship weakens to Rank IC below 0.03 in any 2-year rolling window.
